Visual verification completed.

Dev server is running at `http://127.0.0.1:5173` under process `8004`. Screenshot saved to [production/screenshot-verification.png](C:/Users/oscar/AI%20WORKBENCH/devChorus/automagically-refactored/production/screenshot-verification.png).

Result: the game does **not** render correctly in Chromium. The screenshot shows only the loading overlay: `Loading AutoMagically Built Games...`. Playwright waited for a visible `canvas` for 30 seconds and timed out, so textures were not visible and the Pixi canvas was not attached to the page.

I also checked that `assets/manifest.json` is being served correctly, so this looks more like app/Pixi initialization is not completing in the browser than a missing manifest file.

"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe" -Command "@'
const { chromium } = require('playwright');
(async () => {
  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
  const page = await browser.newPage({ viewport: { width: 1280, height: 720 }, deviceScaleFactor: 1 });
  const errors = [];
  page.on('pageerror', err => errors.push("'`pageerror: ${err.message}`));
  page.on('"'console', msg => {
    if (['error', 'warning'].includes(msg.type())) errors.push("'`${msg.type()}: ${msg.text()}`);
  });
  await page.goto('"'http://127.0.0.1:5173', { waitUntil: 'networkidle', timeout: 30000 });
  await page.waitForSelector('canvas', { state: 'visible', timeout: 30000 });
  await page.waitForTimeout(1500);
  const info = await page.evaluate(() => {
    const canvas = document.querySelector('canvas');
    if ("'!canvas) return { hasCanvas: false };
    const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
    return {
      hasCanvas: true,
      width: canvas.width,
      height: canvas.height,
      clientWidth: rect.width,
      clientHeight: rect.height,
      bodyText: document.body.innerText.slice(0, 500),
      canvases: document.querySelectorAll('"'canvas').length,
    };
  });
  await page.screenshot({ path: 'production/screenshot-verification.png', fullPage: true });
  console.log(JSON.stringify({ url: page.url(), info, errors }, null, 2));
  await browser.close();
})();

const app = new Application();
async function init() {
  await app.init({ resizeTo: window, background: 7784424 });

  const config = await loadConfig();
  const input = new InputManager();
  const scenes = new SceneManager(app.stage);
  scenes.push(

      scenes.replace(new GameScene(app.stage, input, config, getGameplayTextures()));
